arts.ttl: Fans putting phones in my face is bullying move - Postecoglou
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur manager Ange Postecoglou says he "won't accept" fans putting phones in his face because he believes it is a "bullying" tactic.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou gives passionate response about engaging with fans and what Tottenham must do
arts.sum: "If somebody says (picks up phone and holds it up, as if recording), 'Hey, I didn't like that article', what do you think they are trying to achieve? "I think the fact that out of last night the big story out of that is that my interaction with the fans and not the fact a game of football was materially changed by technology, and again I know people will say ‘well, we got to the right decision’, that’s not what I’m talking about.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou echoes Antonio Conte rant by urging Tottenham to ‘stick to something’
arts.sum: Conte claimed in 2023 nothing at Tottenham would change if they continue to get rid of managers

arts.ttl: Postecoglou says Spurs ‘need to stick to something’ to improve club’s outlook
arts.sum: Postecoglou fighting to keep his job after league struggles ‘I just don’t think it’s about the managers themselves’ Ange Postecoglou believes Tottenham must make material changes to their outlook as a club if they are to reach the level of success they crave. The manager, who is fighting to continue into next season, described his job as different to anything he had experienced because of the “weight of things you’re trying to rail against”. They include the isolation of the post, how the Spurs manager is normally the lone spokesperson for the club, nobody from the hierarchy publicly defending its positions. This echoed the sentiments of some of his predecessors, especially Antonio Conte. Postecoglou did not name names but it is a matter of fact that the chairman, Daniel Levy, almost never gives interviews. The chief football officer, Scott Munn, and the technical director, Johan Lange, are similarly low-profile. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: I am the only voice defending Spurs - Postecoglou
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou gives an impassioned, detailed defence of his record while adding he is struggling with unique challenges at Tottenham.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou aims fire at Tottenham's board: Frustrated Spurs boss bemoans hierarchy silence and calls for backing from club legends in Liverpool comparison
arts.sum: Postecoglou called for Spurs to be 'more vocal', which was a pointed reference to the silence from the boardroom and their former players on the punditry circuit.

arts.ttl: 'He didn't look himself': Ange Postecoglou says 'outstanding' Tottenham player was struggling physically last night
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur are having a nightmare end to the season as they fell to another Premier League defeat on Thursday night. Tottenham were beaten 1-0 by Chelsea at...

arts.ttl: Exclusive: Tottenham eyeing Brazilian wonderkid likened to Adriano, big move looking likely this summer
arts.sum: TBR Football can exclusively confirm that Liverpool and Tottenham Hotspur are among the Premier League clubs interested in a Brazilian wonderkid likened to Adriano. Liverpool...

arts.ttl: ‘Watch out’ – Tottenham man could be right name for club to start over and cause domino effect
arts.sum: Guglielmo Vicario impressed for Tottenham again yesterday, as Ange Postecoglou’s side fell to a 1-0 loss to Chelsea. The Italian made a terrific save off Jadon Sancho in the first half and showed his reliable ability on the ball. arts.ttl: “Half of Wembley was signing my name” – Ex-Southampton star looks back on cherished moment, Saints helped him achieve dream
arts.sum: Italian striker Manolo Gabbiadini had a short yet impactful spell at Southampton. He played 60 games and scored 12 times in all competitions. Despite never truly being consistent, he scored a brace for the Saints in the 2017 League Cup final against Manchester United.
